blocks.content: The song ended. She didn't move.
The second time through she stopped pretending she was listening neutrally. She knew who the song was about. She knew who had put it there.
She was crying before she noticed she was.

Not quietly. Not in the careful, composed way she cried at movies or when something was manageable. Hard — the kind that comes up from somewhere low and doesn't ask permission. She pressed the back of her hand to her mouth. The ceiling fan turned. The song kept going.
When it ended she sat there in the quiet with her face wet.
She wiped her face. Looked at the screen.
